data.title: U.S. 'Close' to Signing Trade Deals With Japan and South Korea
data.description:
The U.S. is reportedly "close" to signing trade and tariff deals with Japan and South Korea, according to Fox Business senior correspondent Charles Gasparino in a post on X. Gasparino, citing Wall Street sources close to the White House, added that a U.S.-India deal is also in the works, although it has been slowed down by the country's conflict with Pakistan.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

Additional U.S. Trade Deals Said to Be On the Way

The U.S. currently has a 10% baseline tariff on both Japan and South Korea after reciprocal tariffs on the two countries were paused for 90 days. In 2024, the U.S. had a $68.5 billion goods trade deficit with Japan and a $66 billion goods trade deficit with South Korea.

Despite the news, the iShares MSCI Japan ETF (EWJ) is trading lower while the iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) is in the green.

data.title: China Suspends Export Controls for 28 U.S. Firms, Adjusts Unreliable List Policy for Some U.S. Companies
data.description:
In an encouraging sign of improving relations between the two world powers, China has suspended export controls for 28 U.S. companies, according to Bloomberg. In addition, China has either suspended or completely removed some U.S. companies from its unreliable entities list. The exact companies haven't been specified at the time of writing.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

China Eases on U.S. Trade Control

The action marks a sharp contrast to Beijing's attitude a month ago when it added an additional six companies to its unreliable entities list, which included aerospace and defense company Sierra Nevada Corporation and Shield AI. In March, the country added 15 U.S. companies to its export-control list.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading slightly higher on the development.

data.title: Trump Signs Joint Declaration of Cooperation With Qatar
data.description:
President Trump has a signed a joint declaration of cooperation agreement with Qatar as he continues his journey across the Middle East. Qatar has also agreed to purchase 160 aircrafts from Boeing (BA) worth $200 billion, representing the largest widebody aircraft order in Boeing's history. BA stock is trading higher on the news.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

"We're with you all the way," said Trump to Qatar Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.

Trump Continues Signing Deals in the Middle East

On Tuesday, the President announced a $600 billion investment commitment from Saudi Arabia, benefitting sectors like technology, energy, and defense. As part of the commitment, Saudi company AviLease agreed to buy $4.8 billion of Boeing 737-8 passenger aircraft. In addition, the U.S. will provide the Kingdom with about $142 billion of defense equipment.

data.title: Trump Meets With Syria's President
data.description:
On Tuesday, President Trump stated that he would like to end all U.S. sanctions against Syria, which have been active for over two decades. The President met with Syrian President Ahmed Al-Sharaa today in a meeting presided by Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and joined by Turkey's President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Trump Urges Syrian President to Sign Abraham Accords

During the meeting, Trump encouraged Sharaa to sign the Abraham Accords, which establishes bilateral and diplomatic normalization agreements with Israel. The United Arab Emirates (UAE), Bahrain, Sudan, and Morocco have previously signed the accord. Trump also encouraged the Syrian President to deport terrorists from the country and prevent the Islamic State from regaining power.

"President Trump thanked President Erdogan and the Crown Prince for their friendship, and told President Al-Sharaa that he has a tremendous opportunity to do something historic in his country," said the White House.

The S&P 500 (SPX) has not shown a reaction to the news and is close to unchanged for the day.

data.title: Gold Prices Fall Below $3,200 Amid Lower Inflation and Tariffs
data.description:
The price of spot gold has fallen by about 2% today with an ounce now trading below $3,200. The precious metal, seen as a hedge against inflation and economic uncertainty, is now down about 1% during the past month after rising as much as 6.2% and surpassing $3,400 per ounce.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

Cool CPI Report Contributes to Gold's Fall

Yesterday, the April consumer price index (CPI) report showed inflation of 2.3%, the lowest rate since February 2021 and below the expectation for 2.4%. While that was good news for consumers' pockets, it wasn't for gold. Gold also typically carries an inverse relationship with economic uncertainty, which declined in recent days given tariff reductions between the U.S. and China. The U.S. also reached a trade deal with the UK last week.

Gold has still remained strong on a year-to-date basis. The big question is if these gains will hold, and for how long.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 5/14/25 – Futures Steady After Tech-Driven Rally
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were stable early Wednesday following a strong rally in the technology sector during the previous session. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.01%, 0.1%, and 0.05%, respectively, at 4:23 a.m. EST, May 14.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

This muted activity in futures followed a largely positive day on Wall Street. The S&P 500 gained 0.72%, and the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) surged 1.61%, marking its fifth consecutive day of gains. The upside was fueled by easing U.S.-China trade tensions and encouraging inflation data, which boosted investor sentiment.

However, the Dow Jones declined by 0.64%, primarily due to an 18% drop in UnitedHealth (UNH) stock after the sudden exit of its CEO and outlook suspension.

Technology stocks were top gainers in Tuesday's regular session, with the sector advancing by over 2%. Nvidia (NVDA) stock jumped 5.6% on a major AI chip agreement with Saudi Arabia. Also, Coinbase saw its stock soar by 24% following its inclusion in the S&P 500 index.

On the earnings front, companies such as Cisco (CSCO), Dynatrace (DT), Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), Global-E Online (GLBE), and Nextracker (NXT) will report results today.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.471%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending lower, hovering near $63.50 per barrel as of the last check.

At the same time, the Gold Spot U.S. Dollar Price fell to $3,240 per ounce on Tuesday, as demand faltered amid lower concerns about the U.S.-China trade war.

Elsewhere, European indices opened muted today. The recent rally, driven by easing global trade tensions, appears to be losing momentum as investors reassess market conditions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed Today

Asia-Pacific indices were mixed today, following Wall Street's overnight gains, as investor sentiment improved on easing trade tensions and cooler inflation data. Investors are also watching President Trump's upcoming visit to the Middle East, which coincides with peak fuel demand, making oil price stability a likely priority.

At the same time, the Hong Kong index was up 2.3%. Also, China's Shenzhen Component and Shanghai Composite indices closed higher by 0.64% and 0.86%, respectively. However, Japan's Topix and Nikkei indices declined 0.32% and 0.14%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: TFII Lawsuit Alert! Class Action Lawsuit Against TFI International Inc.
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against TFI International Inc. (TFII) by Levi & Korsinsky on March 14, 2025. The plaintiffs (shareholders) alleged that they bought TFII stock at artificially inflated prices between April 26, 2024 and February 19, 2025 (Class Period) and are now seeking compensation for their financial losses. Investors who bought TFI International stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

TFI International is a Canadian logistics and transportation company, operating through three segments: Less-Than-Truckload (LTL), Truckload, and Logistics. In 2021, TFI acquired UPS Freight, the LTL and dedicated truckload divisions of United Parcel Service (UPS). Following the acquisition, UPS Freight was rebranded as TForce Freight and integrated into the company's LTL segment.

The company's tall claims about revenue and profitability of its TForce Freight business are at the heart of the current complaint.

TFI International's Misleading Claims

According to the lawsuit, TFI and two of its senior officers (Individual Defendants) repeatedly made false and misleading public statements throughout the Class Period. Particularly, they are accused of omitting truthful information about the loss of small and medium business customers, and resulting declines in TForce Freight revenue, from SEC filings and related material.

For instance, during the Class Period, the company reported marginal year-over-year growth in both total revenue and revenue before fuel surcharge items for three different quarterly reports in Fiscal 2024. TFI attributed this growth to "contributions from acquisitions partially offset by a reduction of volumes due to a continued weaker transportation environment and to a reduction in fuel surcharge revenue."

However, subsequent events (discussed below) revealed that TFI misled investors about the gradual loss of its small and medium business customers.

Plaintiffs' Arguments

The plaintiffs maintain that the defendants deceived investors by lying and withholding critical information about the business practices and prospects during the Class Period. Importantly, the defendants are accused of misleading investors about the growth trajectory of the company's TForce business.

The information became clear after the market closed on February 19, 2025, when TFI reported weaker-than-expected results for its fourth quarter and full year Fiscal 2024 period. During the conference call held on the same day, the CEO acknowledged that the company was consistently losing small and medium-sized customers, which had the best margins. This trend accelerated in Q4, significantly impacting TFI's most profitable business. Following the news, TFII stock plunged 20.5% on February 20.

To conclude, the defendants allegedly misled investors regarding challenges in TFI International's largest business segment, TForce Freight, which ultimately impacted the company's overall financial performance. Owing to these issues, TFII stock has lost 32.8% year-to-date, causing massive damage to shareholder returns.

Disclaimer & DisclosureReport an Issue
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market News Today – SPY and QQQ Stock Erase YTD Losses After CPI Report, AI Diffusion Rule Rescission
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) have reversed all of their year-to-date losses as of the market close today in a spectacular turnaround.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

Before the market open, the Bureau of Labor Statistics released its April Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, showing 2.3% inflation. That marked the lowest growth since February 2021 and was below the expectation for 2.4%. The report provided a nice jump-start for the benchmark indices as they opened in the green.

Shortly after, President Trump announced that Saudi Arabia had committed to investing $600 billion in the U.S., supporting a variety of industries that include technology, energy, and defense. Companies mentioned in the commitment include Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Uber (UBER), Boeing (BA), Salesforce (CRM), and several more. Trump is currently in the Middle East and is set to speak with other key leaders in the coming days.

The President also announced that he will lift all U.S. sanctions on Syria in a stunning move. "In Syria, they've had their share of travesty, war, killing many years," said Trump. "That's why my administration has already taken the first steps toward restoring normal relations between the United States and Syria for the first time in more than a decade." Trump is set to meet with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa tomorrow in Riyadh.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Commerce Department has rescinded the AI Diffusion Rule just days before it was set to be enforced. The rule aimed to create a three-tier system for countries receiving U.S. chips. Trump will replace the rule with his own policy, although details have yet to be announced. In the meantime, the Bureau for Industry and Security (BIS) has released guidance for controlling AI chip exports, such as stating that the use of Huawei Ascend chips violates U.S. export controls.

The S&P 500 finished the day with a 0.72% gain while the Nasdaq 100 closed higher by 1.58%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: China Voices Opposition to U.S.-UK Trade Deal
data.description:
China isn't very happy with the U.S-UK trade deal, according to the Financial Times in a new report. In particular, the world's second-largest economy is concerned that the trade deal could exclude Chinese products from British supply chains as a result of "strict security requirements for Britain's steel and pharmaceuticals industries."

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

The news comes after the both the U.S. and China agreed to lower their tariffs on each other by 115% for 90 days.

Trade Agreements Should Not Target Other Countries, Says China

"Co-operation between states should not be conducted against or to the detriment of the interests of third parties," said China's foreign ministry to the FT.

A Chinese government official who requested anonymity noted that China will need to respond to the U.S.-UK deal, although it isn't exactly clear how. "This type of poison pill clause is actually worse than the tariffs," said the official.

The iShares China Large-Cap ETF has remained resilient in the face of the tariff drama with a year-to-date return of 14.42%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Market News Today – SPY and QQQ Stock Rally Higher Following U.S.-China Agreement, Trump Bill Draft
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) closed Monday with solid gains after both the U.S. and China agreed to lower their tariffs on each other by 115% for 90 days.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

While most sectors closed higher, it wasn't pretty for pharmaceutical "middlemen." This morning, Trump signed an executive order aiming to lower drug prices for Americans. Included in the order was a promise to simplify the process of buying drugs directly from manufacturers, cancelling out the middlemen. CVS (CVS), Cigna (CI), and UnitedHealth Group (UNH) closed lower on the news. In addition, Trump seeks to stop the practice of foreign nations receiving lower drug prices than Americans.

"In case after case, our citizens pay massively higher prices than other nations pay for the same exact pill, from the same factory, effectively subsidizing socialism abroad with skyrocketing prices at home," said the President.

Meanwhile, the U.S. April federal budget surplus reached $258.4 billion, ahead of the expectation for $256 billion. The beat was driven by $16 billion in custom duties, up from $9 billion year-over-year, as tariffs began to take effect. A solid tax collection season also contributed to the gains. The deficit for fiscal 2025 so far totals $1.049 trillion.

As for Trump's anticipated bill, House Republicans have released a draft. Included in the text was a proposal to end the EV tax credit early and a tax break for overtime and tips through 2028.

Finally, The New York Times revealed that the U.S. is set to sign chip deals with Saudi Arabia and Emirati AI firm G42, benefitting Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) in the process. The report comes as Trump travels to the Middle East to speak with officials.

The S&P 500 finished Monday higher by 3.26% while the Nasdaq 100 provided a 4.02% gain.
